It's a good rule of thumb to never turn your back on the enemy. For those who forget why, you'll be happy to remind them.

@@#AtkMod:BackStabAtkMod:StatMod:BackStabStatMod@@ when attacking from @@#Color:Red:Behind@@.
BanditArcher_TitlePage:
@@#Color:Red:Bandit Archers@@ are a subspecies of @@#Color:Red:Bandit@@ who specialize in @@#Tag:Physical@@ @@#Tag:Ranged:Hint:Ranged Attacks@@ with a @@#Color:Red:Bow@@.

Through years of grueling training in short shorts, these brave few have learned the secrets of the bare leg; sacrificing @@#Stat:Strength@@ and @@#Stat:Armor@@ in favor of higher @@#Stat:Agility@@ and @@#Stat:Speed@@.

Naturally this makes them best suited for attacking from a distance, but despite all their training @@#Color:Red:they still can't hit a moving target@@.
BanditAxe: An old wood splitting axe repurposed for splitting skulls. Just goes to show that anything can be used as a weapon when already shaped like a weapon.
BanditBase_TitlePage: @@#Color:Red:Bandits@@ are well rounded @@#Tag:Physical@@ fighter who balances offence and defense. They excel in melee brawls, but struggle against @@#Tag:Ranged@@ @@#Tag:Magic:Hint:Magic Attacks@@ and a crushing guilt of their many sins.
BanditCaptainHelmet_HeadApparel: The same cheap metal helmet worn by Bandits, but this one has the scarf on top to show off your pretty mug.
BanditCaptain_TitlePage:
Every ship needs a @@#Color:Red:Captain@@, and while @@#Color:Red:Bandits@@ are not necessarily ships, they do still benefit from well organized leadership.

@@#Color:Red:Bandit Captains@@ earned their @@#Color:Blue:Title@@ thanks to their higher than average @@#Color:Red:Attributes@@. Naturally, they keep the best loot for themselves which means they tend to have better @@#Stat:Armor@@ and @@#Tag:HeavyWeapon:Hint:Heavy Weapons@@.

They often like to live by the motto:
"Carry a big stick, and use it."
BanditHeavyArmor_BodyApparel: A cheap leather shirt reinforced with some iron. Prefect protection for robbing innocents of their livelihood.
BanditHeavyArmor_HeadApparel: The same A cheap metal helmet worn by Bandits.
BanditHeavyArmor_LegApparel: Cheap leather pants reinforced with iron. Someone probably died in these.
BanditLightArmor_BodyApparel: A cheap leather vest. Bad for protection but great for mobility and showing off your guns.
BanditLightArmor_HeadApparel: A pointy hat commonly seen on archers. Apparently it's called a "bycocket".
BanditLightArmor_LegApparel: Cheap shorts with simple leather shoes. Bad for protection but great for mobility and showing off your legs.
Bandit_PageBook: Written and self published by Benjamin "Skull Crusher" Taylors, it was quickly accepted as the standard guide for practical violence among the banditry community.
BarrelActor:
@@#Color:Red:Barrels@@ were first invented as a subsite for @@#Color:Red:Pinatas@@ during the @@#Color:Red:Great Papier-Mache Wars@@. But failed to find popularity among @@#Color:Grey:Children@@ due to their boring appearance and difficulty to break.

After the extinction of @@#Color:Grey:Children@@ and subsequent end to all birthday parties, people have found them useful for storing @@#Tag:Supplies@@ and providing a rustic feel.

Some more nostalgic adventurers still prefer to use them for their original purpose.

BaseSquirrel:
Radical political extremist of the @@#Color:Blue:New Squorld Order@@. These small and squirrely (they hate that term) @@#Tag:Beasts@@ are highly aggressive. While lacking in @@#Stat:MaxHealth@@ and @@#Stat:PhyAttack@@, they have high @@#Stat:Speed@@ and just enough @@#Stat:Evasion@@ to be annoying.

Often attacking in large swarms, the @@Color:Blue:NSO@@ has committed countless acts of terrorism on defenseless @@#Color:Red:Abandoned Villages@@. So don't feel bad about killing them.

@@#Color:Red:Chain Attacks@@ can chain up to @@#StatMod:MaxChainMod:Value@@ additional @@#Color:Red:Actors@@ per use.

@@#Color:Red:Crates@@ are like the older more mature sibling of @@#Color:Red:Barrels@@. While @@#Color:Red:Barrels@@ are off rolling down hills and filling themselves with beer, @@#Color:Red:Crates@@ are working diligently storing items in warehouses and working as makeshift tables.

Many of the more euclidean minded folks will argue that @@#Color:Red:Crates@@ are superior due to their greater volume and packability. But honestly if you're just breaking them it's all them same..

IceKnife_Weapon: It was supposed to be the perfect murder. Stab the victim, knife melts, no evidence. But turns out magic ice doesn't melt, so here we are.
LeverActor:
In an age where buttons and switches have dominated the mechanical control market, many consumers find themselves longing for a more relatable solution. @@#Color:Red:Barrels@@ were first invented as a subsite for @@#Color:Red:Pinatas@@ during the @@#Color:Red:Great Papier-Mache Wars@@. But failed to find popularity among @@#Color:Grey:Children@@ due to their boring appearance and difficulty to break.

After the extinction of @@#Color:Grey:Children@@ and subsequent end to all birthday parties, people have found them useful for storing @@#Tag:Supplies@@ and providing a rustic feel.

Some more nostalgic adventurers still prefer to use them for their original purpose.

LowAggro_PageItem:
It's easy to go unnoticed when in a crowd or alone in an alley. But not drawing attention while stabbing someone in their side, that takes real skill.

Generate less @@#Color:Red:30% less Threat@@ causing Enemies to be less likely to prioritise you as their Target.
MagBoostPage:
The first thing @@#Color:Blue:The Collage@@ teaches every new @@#Color:Blue:Mage@@ is how to optimize @@#Color:Blue:Mag Ammo@@ usage. This leads to more efficient @@#Color:Blue:Spell@@ casting, and frankly saves a ton of @@#Color:Blue:Money@@.

MushroomBase: A common and harmless form of mushroom found throughout the World. Normally the @@#Color:Blue:PAGE@@ System doesn't register inanimate vegetation as @@#Color:Red:Actors@@, but fungi are really closer to animals than plants, so they count.
MushroomRoof: A less common subspecies of mushroom fond of growing on roofs. They are popular for use in cooking and ranged attack tutorials.
Mushroom_TitlePage: Mushroom Title Page. You shouldn't actually have access to this.

OldDagger: A simple dagger dulled from years of various uses. Old, but still works.

ShroomGolem: Despite their name, @@#Color:Red:Shroom Golems@@ are neither @@#Color:Red:Mushrooms@@ nor @@#Color:Red:Golems@@, but instead are categorized as marsupials. This ambush predators tend to disguise themselves as harmless @@#Color:Red:Mushrooms@@, luring in their prey then striking with Poisonous Spores and Bludging Attacks, before robbing them blind to pay for scratch off lottery tickets. They don't expect to win any real money, they just like scratching off that silver foil stuff. Oh ya, and they really hate @@#Color:DmgColor:Light:Light@@.

Skeleton:
A spooky scary @@#Color:Red:Skeleton@@ risen from the dead. This form of reanimation accounts for a lack of muscle tissue by focusing on direct manipulation of bone. This results in an @@#Tag:Undead@@ that is physically weaker, but has the @@#Color:Red:Agility@@ required to use @@#Tag:Ranged:Hint:Ranged Weapons@@.

Thier exposed bone is resistant to @@#Color:DmgColor:Slash@@ and @@#Color:DmgColor:Pierce@@, but is quite vulnerable to @@#Color:DmgColor:Blunt@@ and @@#Color:DmgColor:Crash@@. They often remain on back lines using range to attack from afar. But, while some may view them as nightmarish monsters, I like to believe there's a @@#Color:Red:Skeleton@@ inside us all.
SkeletonMage: A spooky scary @@#Color:Red:Skeleton@@ risen from the dead.
SkeletonMage_TitlePage:
Due to @@#Color:Red:Mages'@@ innate lack of muscle tissue, it's inevitable they will become a @@#Color:Red:Skeleton@@ if turned @@#Tag:Undead@@. Less inevitable, however, is if they can retain their @@#Tag:Spell:Hint:Spell Pages@@ and the @@#Stat:Intelligence@@ required to use them.

Their bare bones body has the same resistances and vulnerabilities any other @@#Color:Red:Skeleton@@, but their @@#Color:Red:Dark Robes@@ provide @@#Stat:Ward@@ and a sense of modisty.

@@#Color:Red:Skeleton Mages@@ are known to be aggressive front-line @@#Tag:Melee@@ fighters relying on high defense and just kidding, they cast @@#Tag:Spell:Hint:Spells@@ from a safe distance.

While some may view them as nightmarish monsters, I like to believe there's a @@#Color:Red:Skeleton@@ inside us all.
SlimeBase_TitlePage:
@@#Color:Red:Slimes@@ are gelatinous blobs of biological paradoxes. They have no discernible sensory organs, digestive system, or really anything resembling "anotomy", yet still act and behave as independent beings.

It was once believed their lack of eyes should render them @@#EftDef:AilmentBlind:Link@@. But instead, the @@#Color:Blue:PAGE@@ System's inability to registering a @@#Color:Red:Front@@ or @@#Color:Red:Back@@ is interpreted as perfect @@#Stat:Awareness@@.

Similarly, their malleable forms have no measurable amount of @@#Stat:Armor@@, yet still demonstrate high @@#Tag:Resistant@@ to all forms of @@#Tag:Physical@@ damage. 

Dispite these abnormalities, researchers have been able to prove that @@#Color:Red:Slimes@@ will die if stabbed enough and thus, meet the scientific definition of "Life". But the council still does not grant them the @@#Color:Blue:Tag@@ of @@#Tag:Living@@.
SlimeBasic: @@#Color:Red:Slimes@@ are gelatinous blobs of biological paradoxes with no discernible sensory organs, digestive system, or really anything resembling "anotomy". It was once believed their lack of eyes should render them @@#EftDef:AilmentBlind:Link@@. But instead, the @@#Color:Blue:PAGE@@ System errors in registering a @@#Color:Red:Front@@ or @@#Color:Red:Back@@ and defaults to perfect @@#Stat:Awareness@@. Similarly, their malleable forms have no measurable amount of @@#Stat:Armor@@, yet still demonstrate high @@#Tag:Resistant@@ to all forms of @@#Tag:Physical@@ damage. Despite these abnormalities, researchers have been able to prove that @@#Color:Red:Slimes@@ will die if stabbed enough and thus, meet the scientific definition of "Life". But the council still does not grant them the @@#Color:Blue:Tag@@ of @@#Tag:Living@@.
SlimePoison:
With a high tolerance to toxins and poor personal hygiene, @@#Color:Red:Slimes@@ often become polluted with whatever is in their environment. After a @@#Color:Red:Slime@@ ingests enough material with @@#Tag:Magical@@ properties, the @@#Color:Blue:PAGE@@ System appears to misinterpret the @@#Color:Red:Slime@@ as possessing the same properties of the given material. At this point, the @@#Color:Red:Slime@@ will alter it's color to conform to cultural norms.

@@#Color:Red:Green Slimes@@ have absorbed large amounts of poisonous plants, toxic waste, and internet comment sections. Any contact with these them is recognized as @@#Color:DmgColor:Poison:Poison Damage@@ and can be potent enough to inflict deadly @@#EftDef:AilmentPoisoned:Link@@ status to the idiot who touched it with their bare hands.

Squirrcerer: Magic wielding specialist of the NSO. They have been trained in the forbidden acornic arts and serve as back line attackers, casting powerful acorn related @@#Color:Red:Spells@@... well, "@@#Color:Red:Spell@@". They only have one. And all it can do is set an acorn on fire which they then throw. So I guess really they are just a @@#Color:Red:Squirrel@@ with a lighter that happens to only works on acorns.
SquirrelAmmo_Bag: A tee-tiny little pouch made from two leaves sewn together. Somehow it's able to fit quite a few clips of ammo.
SquirrelMag_TitlePage:
@@#Color:Red:Squirrcerer@@ are the @@#Tag:Magic@@ users of the @@#Color:Blue:NSO@@.

They have been trained in the forbidden acornic arts granting access to powerful acorn related @@#Tag:Spell:Hint:Spells@@... well, "@@#Color:Blue:Spell@@". They only have one. And all it can do is set an acorn on @@#Color:DmgColor:Fire@@ which they then throw. So I guess really they are just a @@#Color:Red:Squirrel@@ with a lighter that happens to only works on acorns.

Still, they are technically casters so they like to stay on the back line and fair poorly against @@#Tag:Physical:Hint:Physical Attacks@@ or, in their case, being stepped on.
SquirrelPhy_TitlePage:
@@#Color:Red:Squirrior@@ are the @@#Tag:Armor:Hint:Heavy Armored@@ defenders of the @@#Color:Blue:NSO@@.

They sacrifice their natural squirrelish @@#Stat:Evasion@@ for @@#Stat:Armor@@ to stand as the front line defense.

Following the emergence of the @@#Color:Blue:NSO@@, @@#Color:Blue:The Military@@ commissioned researchers at @@#Color:Blue:The Collage@@ to uncover how these tiny fighters were able to achieve such high @@#Color:Red:Armor@@ with such tiny @@#Tag:Appearal@@. After decades of grant funded research, they concluded: "Probably Magic."
Squirrel_PageBook: A political manifesto carried by all members of The New Squirrel Order. It preaches the idea of ultimate squrrpremacy over all other @@#Color:Red:Factions@@ and is popular among young rodents who struggle at dating. Mandatory for defining the @@#Color:Red:Squirrel@@ you are.
Squirrior: Heavy armored defenders of the NSO. They sacrifice their natural squirrelish @@#Color:Red:Evasion@@ for @@#Color:Red:Armor@@ to stand as the front line defense. Following the emergence of the NSO, @@#Color:Red:The Military@@ commissioned researchers at @@#Color:Red:The Collage@@ to uncover how these tiny fighters were able to achieve such high @@#Color:Red:Armor@@ with platemail thinner than a tin can. After decades of study, the researchers concluded: "Idk, probably Magic."
StalagmiteActor:
@@#Color:Red:Crates@@ are like the older more mature sibling of @@#Color:Red:Barrels@@. While @@#Color:Red:Barrels@@ are off rolling down hills and filling themselves with beer, @@#Color:Red:Crates@@ are working diligently storing items in warehouses and working as makeshift tables.

Many of the more euclidean minded folks will argue that @@#Color:Red:Crates@@ are superior due to their greater volume and packability. But honestly if you're just breaking them it's all them same..

ZombieBasic: A common form of @@#Tag:Undead@@ created through the reanimation of a @@#Color:Red:Corpse@@. @@#Color:Red:Zombies@@ are peaceful and docile beings almost never resorting to violence, except towards all living creatures whom they will relentlessly attack on sight. They have an innate love of helicopters, gleefully chasing after any they see fly by. But sadly, no one ever let's them ride in one.

Zombie_TitlePage:
@@#Color:Red:Zombies@@ are a common form of @@#Tag:Undead@@ created through the reanimation of a @@#Color:Red:Corpse@@. They are peaceful beings almost never resorting to violence, except towards all @@#Tag:Living@@ creatures whom they relentlessly attack on sight.

Basic @@#Color:Red:Zombies@@ are @@#Tag:Unarmed@@ and mostly rely on weak punches. But when they're feeling feisty, they can steal @@#Stat:Health@@ with a @@#Color:Red:Bite Attack@@. Paired with their high @@#Stat:HealthMax@@ they can make for effective meat shields.

@@#Color:Red:Zombies@@ have an innate love of helicopters, gleefully chasing after any they see fly by. Sadly, no one ever lets them ride in one.
